title: Removing Residual Background
author: Jonathan Houseley
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bntameie

steps.title: Wash at Room temperature in a...
steps.instruction: Wash at Room temperature in a plastic box on a rocker with copious 0.1× SSC 0.1% SDS for at least 01:00:00 . Sometimes membranes have a residual smeary background that interferes with the signal. This is more common with large membranes or when two membranes are probed in one bottle. If this wash fails, use a paper towel to scrub the membrane on both sides while it is under the SSC/SDS—repeat until no more counts come off onto the paper towel. This will not damage the RNA or the probe and removes residual radioactive gunk that precipitates out of the hybridization buffer.
